# Getting Started with Bolt Design System ## Overview The Bolt Design System (`@blitz/design-system`) is a React component library built for the Bolt product. It provides a set of accessible, themeable UI components built on top of Radix UI primitives with UnoCSS utility-class styling. **Tech Stack:** - React 18 or 19 (peer dependency) - UnoCSS for utility classes and token-based color utilities - Radix UI for accessible component primitives - class-variance-authority (CVA) for variant-based component styling - framer-motion for animations - tailwind-merge for class merging ## Installation ```bash npm install @blitz/design-system ``` ### Peer Dependencies Ensure React 18 or React 19 is installed: ```bash npm install react@^18.0 react-dom@^18.0 # or npm install react@^19.0 react-dom@^19.0 ``` ## Setup ### 1. Import Stylesheets At the root of your application (e.g. `main.tsx` or `App.tsx`), import the required stylesheets: ```ts // UnoCSS theme tokens (CSS custom properties for light and dark themes) import '@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme.css'; // Prebuilt icon SVGs bundled into CSS classes import '@blitz/design-system/icons.css'; ``` ### 2. Configure UnoCSS The design system ships a UnoCSS theme extension. Import `unoThemeColors` and add it to your `uno.config.ts`: ```ts // uno.config.ts import { defineConfig } from 'unocss'; import { unoThemeColors } from '@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme'; export default defineConfig({ theme: { colors: { 'bolt-ds': unoThemeColors, }, }, }); ``` This enables UnoCSS utility classes like `bg-bolt-ds-brand`, `text-bolt-ds-textPrimary`, `border-bolt-ds-borderOutline`, etc. ### 3. Wrap with BoltDesignSystemProvider Wrap your application root with `BoltDesignSystemProvider` to enable tooltip functionality and any future design system context: ```tsx import { BoltDesignSystemProvider } from '@blitz/design-system'; function App() { return ( {/* Your application */} ); } ``` The provider is required when using `Tooltip` or `Button` with `tooltip` prop. You can pass tooltip configuration via the `tooltip` prop: ```tsx ``` ### 4. Set the Theme Apply a theme by setting the `data-theme` attribute on the root element (usually `` or ``): ```html ``` You can also toggle programmatically: ```ts document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', 'dark'); ``` The `.dark` class is also supported (useful with Tailwind dark mode): ```html ``` ## Basic Usage ```tsx import { Button, Input, Badge, BoltDesignSystemProvider } from '@blitz/design-system'; export function App() { return (

Hello, Bolt!

Active
); } ``` ## Available Components The design system ships 16 components: **Atoms:** Alert, Avatar, Badge, Button, Checkbox, DefaultLoader, Dropdown, IconDecoration, Input, Select, Switch, Tooltip **Organisms:** Dialog, OptionCard, OptionCardGroup, Table ## Package Exports | Export path | Description | |---|---| | `@blitz/design-system` | All components and utilities (main entry) | | `@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme` | JS object `unoThemeColors` for UnoCSS config | | `@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme.css` | CSS custom properties for UnoCSS | | `@blitz/design-system/tokens/tailwind/v3.css` | Tailwind v3 compatible tokens | | `@blitz/design-system/tokens/tailwind/v4.css` | Tailwind v4 `@theme` tokens | | `@blitz/design-system/icons.css` | Prebuilt icon CSS classes | | `@blitz/design-system/styles/reset.css` | CSS reset stylesheet | ## Important Notes - **UnoCSS is required** for components to render correctly. Components use UnoCSS-specific utility classes (e.g., `op-50`) that are not available in standard Tailwind CSS. - Tailwind v3 and v4 token exports exist for token consumption only â€” do **not** use them for component styling. - The package is ESM-only (`"type": "module"`). - **BoltDesignSystemProvider** should wrap your app root to enable tooltips and future context features.

# Foundations â€” Colors ## Overview Bolt's color system is inspired by **Material Design 3** token philosophy. It uses semantic tokens that map to a set of primitive color scales. Tokens are adaptive â€” they automatically change between light and dark themes based on the `data-theme` attribute on the root element. All design tokens are CSS custom properties prefixed with `--bolt-ds-*`. In UnoCSS, they are available as color utilities: `bg-bolt-ds-`, `text-bolt-ds-`, `border-bolt-ds-`, etc. ## Color Primitives These are the raw color scales from which semantic tokens are derived: ### Gray Scale | Step | Value | |------|-------| | 0 | `#ffffff` | | 50 | `#fafafa` | | 100 | `#f5f5f5` | | 200 | `#e5e5e5` | | 300 | `#d4d4d4` | | 400 | `#a3a3a3` | | 500 | `#737373` | | 600 | `#525252` | | 700 | `#404040` | | 800 | `#262626` | | 900 | `#171717` | | 950 | `#101010` | ### Accent Scale (Blue â€” Brand Color) | Step | Value | |------|-------| | 50 | `#eef9ff` | | 100 | `#d8f1ff` | | 200 | `#bae7ff` | | 300 | `#8adaff` | | 400 | `#53c4ff` | | 500 | `#2ba6ff` | | 600 | `#1488fc` | | 700 | `#0d6fe8` | | 800 | `#1259bb` | | 900 | `#154e93` | | 950 | `#122f59` | ### Neutral Scale (Blue-tinted gray) | Step | Value | |------|-------| | 0 | `#fefeff` | | 50 | `#fafafd` | | 100 | `#f3f3f8` | | 200 | `#e9e9f0` | | 300 | `#d4d4dd` | | 400 | `#a3a3ac` | | 500 | `#73737b` | | 600 | `#525258` | | 700 | `#3c3c41` | | 800 | `#2c2c30` | | 900 | `#1e1e21` | | 950 | `#111114` | ### Green Scale (Success) | Step | Value | |------|-------| | 50 | `#f0fdf4` | | 100 | `#dcfce7` | | 200 | `#bbf7d0` | | 300 | `#86efac` | | 400 | `#4ade80` | | 500 | `#22c55e` | | 600 | `#16a34a` | | 700 | `#15803d` | | 800 | `#166534` | | 900 | `#14532d` | | 950 | `#052e16` | ### Orange Scale (Warning) | Step | Value | |------|-------| | 50 | `#fff7ed` | | 100 | `#ffedd5` | | 200 | `#fed7aa` | | 300 | `#fdba74` | | 400 | `#fb923c` | | 500 | `#f97316` | | 600 | `#ea580c` | | 700 | `#c2410c` | | 800 | `#9a3412` | | 900 | `#7c2d12` | | 950 | `#431407` | ### Red Scale (Danger) | Step | Value | |------|-------| | 50 | `#fef2f2` | | 100 | `#fee2e2` | | 200 | `#fecaca` | | 300 | `#fca5a5` | | 400 | `#f87171` | | 500 | `#ef4444` | | 600 | `#dc2626` | | 700 | `#b91c1c` | | 800 | `#991b1b` | | 900 | `#7f1d1d` | | 950 | `#450a0a` | ## Semantic Token Categories Semantic tokens are adaptive and change value based on the active theme. There are 8 categories with 46 tokens total. ### Token Naming Pattern (Material Design 3 inspired) - `--bolt-ds-` â€” Primary color for a category - `--bolt-ds-Highlight` â€” Lighter/brighter variation for hover states - `--bolt-ds-on` â€” Text/icons placed ON the primary color (contrast pair) - `--bolt-ds-Container` â€” Background container color (lighter, for cards/badges) - The `rgb()` wrapper is needed for RGB-formatted tokens.

# Foundations â€” Colors

Inspired by Material Design 3. All tokens: `--bolt-ds-*`. UnoCSS utilities: `bg-bolt-ds-<token>`, `text-bolt-ds-<token>`.

Theme: `data-theme="light"` or `data-theme="dark"` on root element.

## Token naming pattern
- `<color>` â€” primary color
- `<color>Highlight` â€” lighter/hover variant
- `on<Color>` â€” text on primary color
- `<color>Container` â€” background container
- `on<Color>Container` â€” text on container
- `<color>Outline` â€” transparent border

## All 46 semantic tokens (light / dark)

**Brand:** brand `#2ba6ff`/`#1488fc`, brandHighlight `#53c4ff`/`#2ba6ff`, onBrand `#fff`/`#eef9ff`, brandContainer `#eef9ff`/`#2ba6ff1a`, onBrandContainer `#122f59`/`#2ba6ff`, brandOutline `#2ba6ff33`/`#2ba6ff1a`

**Surface:** surface `#fefeff`/`#111114`, surfaceAltLight `#f3f3f8`/`#1e1e21`, surfaceAltDark `#f3f3f8`/`#111114`, surfaceOne `#fefeff`/`#1e1e21`, surfaceTwo `#e9e9f0`/`#2c2c30`, surfaceThree `#d4d4dd`/`#2c2c30`, surfaceHighlight `#0000000d`/`#ffffff14`, inverseSurface `#1e1e21`/`#fefeff`, inverseOnSurface `#fefeff`/`#1e1e21`

**Text & Icons:** textPrimary `#111114`/`#fefeff`, textSecondary `#0a0a0abf`/`#ffffffbf`, textTertiary `#0a0a0a99`/`#ffffff99`, iconPrimary `#111114`/`#fefeff`, iconSecondary `#0a0a0a80`/`#ffffff80`

**Borders:** borderOutline `#0000001a`/`#ffffff1a`, borderDivider `#00000026`/`#ffffff26`

**Success:** success `#22c55e`/`#16a34a`, successHighlight `#4ade80`/`#22c55e`, onSuccess `#f0fdf4`/`#f0fdf4`, successContainer `#f0fdf4`/`#22c55e1a`, onSuccessContainer `#052e16`/`#22c55e`, successOutline `#22c55e33`/`#22c55e1a`

**Warning:** warning `#f97316`/`#ea580c`, warningHighlight `#fb923c`/`#f97316`, onWarning `#fff7ed`/`#fff7ed`, warningContainer `#fff7ed`/`#f973161a`, onWarningContainer `#431407`/`#f97316`, warningOutline `#f9731633`/`#f973161a`

**Danger:** danger `#ef4444`/`#ef4444`, dangerHighlight `#f87171`/`#f87171`, onDanger `#fef2f2`/`#fef2f2`, dangerContainer `#fef2f2`/`#ef44441a`, onDangerContainer `#450a0a`/`#f87171`, dangerOutline `#ef444433`/`#ef44441a`

**Neutral:** neutral `#525258`/`#3c3c41`, neutralHighlight `#a3a3ac`/`#73737b`, onNeutral `#fefeff`/`#f3f3f8`, neutralContainer `#fafafd`/`#1e1e21`, onNeutralContainer `#111114`/`#a3a3ac`, neutralOutline `#1111141a`/`#ffffff0d`

foundations--copywriting.full:
# Foundations â€” Copywriting

Tone of voice and UX writing guidelines. The single source of truth for how Bolt speaks to users across all touchpoints.

## Brand personality

Bolt is empowering, confident, and approachable. We talk to builders â€” people with ideas who want to move fast.

**We are:**
- **Empowering** â€” we put the user's vision first, not our technology
- **Confident** â€” we know our product is good, no need to oversell
- **Direct** â€” we respect people's time and get to the point
- **Warm** â€” we're friendly without being performative
- **Inclusive** â€” we write for everyone, regardless of technical background

**We are not:**
- Hype-driven or startup-bro ("LFG!", "10x your workflow!")
- Condescending or overly didactic
- Robotic or corporate ("Please be advised that...")
- Excessively cute or quirky at the expense of clarity

## Voice vs tone

Voice is constant â€” it's who Bolt is. Tone adapts to context.

| Context | Tone | Example |
|---------|------|---------|
| Onboarding | Encouraging, light | "Nice â€” your app is live. Share it with the world." |
| Error state | Calm, helpful, actionable | "Something went wrong. Try again, or check the status page." |
| Destructive action | Serious, clear | "This will permanently delete your project. This can't be undone." |
| Empty state | Motivating, not guilt-tripping | "No projects yet. Start building something." |
| Success | Celebratory but brief | "Deployed! Your app is live." |
| Settings / admin | Neutral, informative | "Set a username for your workspace profile page." |

## Pronouns and addressing users

Write from the user's perspective. Bolt is a tool in their hands, not the main character.

### Use "you" to address the user
- Do: "Your app is ready to deploy."
- Don't: "We've prepared your app for deployment."

### Use "we" sparingly
Reserve "we" for moments where Bolt (the company/team) is genuinely the actor â€” like policy decisions, announcements, or support. Avoid "we" in product UI.
- Do: "We're working on a fix." (status page), "Deploy your project." (button)
- Don't: "We'll deploy your project for you." (UI)

### Avoid "I"
Bolt doesn't have a first-person voice in the product. The AI chat may use "I" naturally, but chrome UI, settings, navigation, and system messages should not.

### Third-person references
- Do: "Bolt handles the deployment."
- Don't: "The platform will handle the deployment for you."

## Capitalization

Use sentence case everywhere. Capitalize only the first word and proper nouns.

Sentence case applies to: page titles, section headings, button labels, navigation items, tab labels, table column headers, tooltips, error/success messages, modal titles, and empty states.

**Do:**
- "Workspace settings"
- "Leave workspace"
- "Set username"
- "Default monthly member credit limit"

**Don't:**
- "Workspace Settings"
- "Leave Workspace"
- "Set Username"
- "Default Monthly Member Credit Limit"

**Why not title case?**
- Sentence case reads more naturally and scans faster
- Eliminates ambiguity about which words get capitalized
- Leading design systems (Google Material, Apple HIG, Atlassian, Shopify Polaris, GitLab Pajamas) have adopted sentence case
- Feels less formal and more aligned with how people actually read interfaces

**Exceptions:**
- **Proper nouns** are always capitalized: Bolt, Claude, GitHub, Supabase, React
- **Acronyms** stay uppercase: API, URL, SEO, CTA, AI
- **Branded features**: Bolt Cloud, WebContainers

## UX writing principles

Core rules for writing clear, scannable UI copy.

**1. Lead with the action or outcome**
- Do: "Deploy to a custom domain"
- Don't: "In order to use a custom domain, you need to deploy first"

**2. Be specific over generic**
- Do: "Connect your database", "Invite a teammate"
- Don't: "Manage database configuration", "Manage team members"

**3. Keep it short**
Every word costs the user's attention. Aim for the fewest words that preserve full clarity.
- Do: "No projects yet"
- Don't: "You haven't created any projects yet. Get started by clicking the button below."

**4. Write for scanning, not reading**
Users don't read UIs â€” they scan them. Front-load the most important word in every label, heading, and message.
- Do: "Token usage this month"
- Don't: "This month's summary of your token usage"

**5. Use the active voice**
- Do: "Enter your workspace name"
- Don't: "Your workspace name should be entered below"

**6. Avoid double negatives**
- Do: "Keep me signed in"
- Don't: "Don't sign me out"

**7. One thought per message**
Don't overload a single UI string with multiple ideas. If you need more context, use helper text or progressive disclosure.

## Buttons and CTAs

Rules for writing clear, action-oriented button labels:
- Use sentence case: "Get started" not "Get Started"
- Lead with a verb: "Deploy project", "Add member", "Set username"
- Be specific: "Save changes" is better than "Submit"
- Skip articles: "Add member" not "Add a member"
- No periods or exclamation marks
- Keep it to 1â€“3 words when possible

| Action | Do | Don't |
|--------|----|-------|
| Primary action | Get started | Get Started! |
| Save | Save changes | Submit |
| Delete | Delete project | Remove |
| Cancel | Cancel | Go back |
| Create | Create project | New project |
| Auth | Sign in / Sign up | Log in / Register |
| Upgrade | Upgrade plan | Click here to upgrade |

### Destructive actions
For irreversible actions, be explicit in the button and add confirmation context:
- Button: **"Delete project"**
- Confirmation: **"This will permanently delete _Project name_ and all its data. This can't be undone."**
- Confirm button: **"Yes, delete project"**
- Cancel: **"Cancel"**

## Error messages

**Formula:** What happened + what to do next. Optionally, why it happened.

**Do:**
- "Couldn't deploy. Check your build logs for errors."
- "This email is already in use. Try signing in instead."

**Don't:**
- "Error: 500 Internal Server Error"
- "Something went wrong."
- "Oops! Looks like something broke."

**Rules:**
- Don't blame the user: "Couldn't save" not "You forgot to fill in the name"
- Don't be overly apologetic: one "sorry" is fine in genuine failure, skip it for validation
- Be specific: "Name must be 50 characters or fewer" not "Invalid input"
- Always give a next step when possible
- Skip technical jargon unless it's a developer-facing context

## Empty states

Empty states are an opportunity, not a dead end. Give the user a path forward.

Structure: **Short headline** + **brief context** (optional) + **CTA**

**Do:**
- "No projects yet"
- "Start building something amazing."
- [Create project]

**Don't:**
- "It looks like you don't have any projects"
- "You haven't created any projects yet. To get started, click the button below..."
- "[Create Your First Project]"

## Numbers and formatting

- Use numerals for all numbers: "3 projects", "10 tokens", "1 collaborator"
- Use "K" and "M" for large numbers: "1.2M tokens", "26M"
- Dates: "Feb 13, 2026" â€” avoid purely numeric formats (ambiguous across locales)
- Time: use 12-hour format with AM/PM for user-facing UI
- Currency: "$25/month" not "$25.00/mo"
- Percentages: "98% less errors" not "ninety-eight percent fewer errors"

## Punctuation

- **No periods** on single-sentence UI strings (buttons, labels, headings, toasts)
- **Use periods** when there are two or more sentences together
- **No exclamation marks** in product UI â€” save them for marketing or celebratory moments, used sparingly
- **Oxford comma**: "hosting, databases, and integrations"
- **Em dashes** (â€”) for breaks in thought, not hyphens (-) or en dashes (â€“)
- **Ellipsis**: use "â€¦" for truncation, never for trailing off ("Loadingâ€¦" is fine; "Wellâ€¦" is not)

## Inclusive language

- Default to gender-neutral language: "they/them" for unknown users, "teammate" not "manpower"
- Don't assume technical proficiency â€” if writing for a broad audience, skip jargon
- Avoid ableist language: "check" not "see", "select" not "hit", "review" not "look at"
- Write for internationalization: avoid idioms, culturally specific references, and humor that doesn't translate

## Terminology

Use consistent terms across the product. Pick one word and stick with it.

| Use | Avoid |
|-----|-------|
| Project | App, application, site |
| Deploy | Ship, launch |
| Token | Credit, unit |
| Agent | Model, LLM, AI engine |
| Sign in / Sign up | Log in / Register / Create account |

## Quick reference checklist

Run through this before shipping any copy:

- [ ] Is it sentence case?
- [ ] Does it lead with the action or outcome?
- [ ] Can it be shorter without losing clarity?
- [ ] Does it use "you" (not "we" or "I")?
- [ ] Is the terminology consistent with the product glossary?
- [ ] Is the tone appropriate for the context (error? success? destructive?)?
- [ ] Is it accessible and jargon-free for the intended audience?
- [ ] Does it give the user a clear next step?
foundations--copywriting.condensed:
# Foundations â€” Copywriting

## Brand personality
Empowering, Confident, Direct, Warm, Inclusive. NOT hype-driven, robotic, or overly cute.

## Key rules
- Use **sentence case** everywhere (not title case). Exceptions: proper nouns (Bolt, GitHub), acronyms (API, URL), branded features
- Address users as **"you"**. Use "we" only for company-level communications, not product UI
- **Lead with action/outcome**, be specific, keep it short, write for scanning
- Use **active voice**, avoid double negatives, one thought per message

## Buttons/CTAs
- Verb-first, sentence case, 1-3 words: "Get started", "Save changes", "Delete project"
- No periods or exclamation marks
- Sign in/Sign up (not Log in/Register)

## Errors: what happened + next step
- "Couldn't deploy. Check your build logs."
- Not: "Something went wrong."

## Empty states: headline + optional context + CTA
- "No projects yet" â†’ "Start building something." â†’ [Create project]

## Punctuation
- No period on single-sentence UI strings; periods when 2+ sentences
- Oxford comma; em dash (â€”) not hyphen; ellipsis for truncation only

## Terminology
Project (not app), Deploy (not ship), Token (not credit), Agent (not model/LLM), Sign in/up (not Log in/Register)

foundations--icons.full:
# Foundations â€” Icons

## Overview

Bolt uses UnoCSS-based icon classes via the `@unocss/preset-icons` approach. Icons render as CSS-masked SVG elements using `currentColor` so they respect the text color of their parent.

There are two icon sources in the design system:
1. **Prebuilt icon classes** â€” 5 icons bundled in `icons.css`, always available without UnoCSS setup
2. **UnoCSS dynamic icon classes** â€” Access to icon sets like Phosphor Icons (`i-ph:*`) and Heroicons (`i-heroicons:*`), available when using UnoCSS with the icons preset

## Prebuilt Icons (icons.css)

These 5 icons are bundled into `@blitz/design-system/icons.css` and are available immediately after importing:

| Class | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `i-bolt:check` | Checkmark (20Ã—20px, filled) |
| `i-bolt:chevron-down` | Chevron pointing down (24Ã—24px) |
| `i-bolt:chevron-up` | Chevron pointing up (24Ã—24px) |
| `i-bolt:minus` | Minus/dash (20Ã—20px, filled) |
| `i-svg-spinners:90-ring-with-bg` | Animated 90-degree loading ring with background ring (24Ã—24px, animated) |

Usage:

```tsx
// Import the icons stylesheet once at app entry
import '@blitz/design-system/icons.css';

// Then use as inline elements
<span className="i-bolt:check text-bolt-ds-success w-4 h-4" />
<span className="i-bolt:chevron-down text-bolt-ds-iconPrimary w-6 h-6" />
<span className="i-bolt:chevron-up text-bolt-ds-iconPrimary w-6 h-6" />
<span className="i-bolt:minus text-bolt-ds-iconSecondary w-4 h-4" />
<span className="i-svg-spinners:90-ring-with-bg text-bolt-ds-brand w-6 h-6" />
```

## UnoCSS Dynamic Icon Classes

When using UnoCSS with the icons preset (e.g., `@unocss/preset-icons`), you get access to any icon from the Iconify ecosystem. Components in the design system use these icon sets:

### Phosphor Icons (`i-ph:*`)

Phosphor Icons are used extensively by components for UI icons. Add the `@iconify-json/ph` package to your project to enable them:

```bash
npm install @iconify-json/ph
```

Example classes used in components:
```tsx
<span className="i-ph:warning-circle" />
<span className="i-ph:check-circle" />
<span className="i-ph:info" />
<span className="i-ph:x-circle" />
<span className="i-ph:user" />
<span className="i-ph:eye" />
<span className="i-ph:eye-slash" />
```

### Heroicons (`i-heroicons:*`)

Some components also reference Heroicons. Add `@iconify-json/heroicons` if needed:

```bash
npm install @iconify-json/heroicons
```

## UnoCSS Configuration for Icons

To use dynamic icon classes (beyond the prebuilt icons.css), configure UnoCSS with the icons preset:

```ts
// uno.config.ts
import { defineConfig, presetIcons } from 'unocss';
import { unoThemeColors } from '@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme';

export default defineConfig({
  presets: [
    presetIcons({
      scale: 1.2,
      warn: true,
    }),
  ],
  theme: {
    colors: {
      'bolt-ds': unoThemeColors,
    },
  },
});
```

## Icon Sizing and Coloring

Icons inherit size from their `width`/`height` CSS properties and color from `currentColor`. Override them with UnoCSS utilities:

```tsx
// Sizing
<span className="i-bolt:check w-4 h-4" />   {/* 16px */}
<span className="i-bolt:check w-5 h-5" />   {/* 20px */}
<span className="i-bolt:check w-6 h-6" />   {/* 24px */}

// Coloring
<span className="i-bolt:check text-bolt-ds-success" />
<span className="i-bolt:check text-bolt-ds-brand" />
<span className="i-bolt:chevron-down text-bolt-ds-iconPrimary" />
<span className="i-bolt:chevron-down text-bolt-ds-iconSecondary" />
```

## Implementation Details

Icons in `icons.css` work via CSS masking. They use:
- `--un-icon`: A `url()` data URI for the SVG
- `-webkit-mask` / `mask`: CSS mask with the icon URL
- `background-color: currentColor`: Inherits text color
- Default dimensions from the viewBox (1em or 24px)

This means icon colors follow the CSS `color` property of their parent or the element itself.

## Prebuilt icons (import `@blitz/design-system/icons.css`)

foundations--spacing.full:
# Spacing

Spacing scale and component sizing for the Bolt Design System.

## Spacing Scale

4px base unit. Use UnoCSS (Tailwind-compatible) utility classes for spacing.

| Class | Value |
|-------|-------|
| `p-0` | 0px |
| `p-0.5` | 2px |
| `p-1` | 4px |
| `p-2` | 8px |
| `p-3` | 12px |
| `p-4` | 16px |
| `p-5` | 20px |
| `p-6` | 24px |
| `p-8` | 32px |
| `p-10` | 40px |
| `p-12` | 48px |
| `p-16` | 64px |

The same scale applies to margin (`m-*`), gap (`gap-*`), and other spacing utilities.

## Component Sizing

Standard sizes for buttons, inputs, and interactive elements.

| Size | Height | Padding | Icon Size |
|------|--------|---------|----------|
| `sm` | 32px | 8px 12px | 16px |
| `md` | 40px | 12px 16px | 20px |
| `lg` | 48px | 16px 20px | 24px |

Most interactive components (Button, Input, Select) support `sm`, `md`, and `lg` sizes. Some also support `xs` (smaller than `sm`) and `xl` (larger than `lg`).

## Usage Guidelines

- Use the 4px grid consistently for all layouts and components.
- Prefer spacing tokens (`p-4`, `gap-6`, etc.) over arbitrary pixel values.
- Internal component padding typically uses `p-2` to `p-4` for compact areas and `p-6` to `p-8` for larger content areas.
- Use `gap-*` for flex and grid layouts rather than margin on individual children.

theming.condensed:
# Theming

Two themes: **light** and **dark**. Default: **dark**.

## Switching

```html
<html data-theme="dark">  <!-- or "light" -->
```

```ts
document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', 'light');
```

## UnoCSS Config

```ts
export default defineConfig({
  theme: { colors: unoThemeColors },
  dark: {
    light: '[data-theme="light"]',
    dark: '[data-theme="dark"]',
  },
});
```

## CSS Structure

- Light: `:root, :root[data-theme='light'] { ... }`
- Dark: `.dark, :root[data-theme='dark'] { ... }`
- Tokens prefixed: `--bolt-ds-*`
- Import: `import '@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme.css'`

## Customizing Colors

```css
:root[data-theme='dark'] {
  --bolt-ds-brand: 120 80 255; /* RGB triplet, no rgb() wrapper */
}
```

## Key Token Values (Dark / Light)

- `--bolt-ds-brand`: `20 136 252` / `43 166 255`
- `--bolt-ds-surface`: `17 17 20` / `254 254 255`
- `--bolt-ds-textPrimary`: `254 254 255` / `17 17 20`
- `--bolt-ds-surfaceOne`: `30 30 33` / `254 254 255`

components--overview.full:
# Components Overview

The Bolt Design System provides 16 React components built on Radix UI primitives and styled with UnoCSS.

## Installation & Setup

```bash
npm install @blitz/design-system
```

Import required stylesheets at your app entry point:

```ts
import '@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme.css';
import '@blitz/design-system/icons.css';
```

Wrap your app with the provider:

```tsx
import { BoltDesignSystemProvider } from '@blitz/design-system';

<BoltDesignSystemProvider>
  {/* Your app */}
</BoltDesignSystemProvider>
```

Configure UnoCSS with the design system color tokens:

```ts
// uno.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from 'unocss';
import { unoThemeColors } from '@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme';

export default defineConfig({
  theme: {
    colors: unoThemeColors,
  },
  dark: {
    light: '[data-theme="light"]',
    dark: '[data-theme="dark"]',
  },
});
```

## Importing Components

All components are exported from the main package:

```ts
import { ComponentName } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

Examples:

```ts
import { Button, Input, Dialog, Table, Alert, OptionCard } from '@blitz/design-system';
import * as Tooltip from '@blitz/design-system'; // Tooltip uses namespace export
```

## Component List

### Atoms (simple, standalone)

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Alert** | Feedback banners with types: neutral, info, success, warning, danger |
| **Avatar** | User profile image or initials/icon fallback |
| **Badge** | Status or category label pill |
| **Button** | Primary interactive element with multiple variants and sizes |
| **Checkbox** | Boolean toggle built on Radix Checkbox |
| **DefaultLoader** | Spinner/loading indicator (also used inside Button) |
| **Dropdown** | Context menu with trigger and item slots |
| **IconDecoration** | Styled icon container with shape and color variants |
| **Input** | Text input with label, hint, error, and warning states |
| **Select** | Dropdown select with label, error support, and ghost variant |
| **Switch** | Toggle switch built on Radix Switch |
| **Tooltip** | Hover tooltip built on Radix Tooltip (namespace export) |

### Organisms (complex, composed)

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Dialog** | Modal dialog with header, content, and action sub-components |
| **OptionCard** | Card for displaying settings/choices with title, description, and action |
| **OptionCardGroup** | Wrapper for OptionCards with dividers between items |
| **Table** | Data table with header, body, footer, row, and cell sub-components |

## Provider

**BoltDesignSystemProvider** wraps the application to supply required context (currently Radix Tooltip provider). Required when using `Tooltip` or `Button` with `tooltip` prop.

## Technical Foundation

- **Radix UI**: Provides accessible primitives for Checkbox, Dialog, Dropdown, Select, Switch, Tooltip.
- **UnoCSS**: Required styling engine. Components use UnoCSS-specific classes (e.g., `op-50`, `i-bolt:*`).
- **class-variance-authority (CVA)**: Used for variant-based class generation (Button, IconDecoration).
- **framer-motion**: Used for animations in Dialog and other animated components.

## Icon Classes

Components use icon classes in the format `i-ph:icon-name` (Phosphor icons) and `i-bolt:icon-name` (bundled icons).

Bundled icons available in `icons.css`:
- `i-bolt:check`
- `i-bolt:chevron-down`
- `i-bolt:chevron-up`
- `i-bolt:minus`
- `i-svg-spinners:90-ring-with-bg`

For other icons, use UnoCSS icon preset with Phosphor icons (`@iconify-json/ph`).
components--overview.condensed:
# Components Overview

Package: `@blitz/design-system`. 16 React components on Radix UI + UnoCSS.

## Setup

```ts
import '@blitz/design-system/tokens/unocss/theme.css';
import '@blitz/design-system/icons.css';
```

```tsx
import { BoltDesignSystemProvider, Button, Input, Alert, Dialog, Table, OptionCard } from '@blitz/design-system';
import * as Tooltip from '@blitz/design-system';

// Wrap app with provider (required for tooltips):
<BoltDesignSystemProvider>{/* app */}</BoltDesignSystemProvider>
```

## Components

**Atoms**: Alert, Avatar, Badge, Button, Checkbox, DefaultLoader, Dropdown, IconDecoration, Input, Select, Switch, Tooltip

**Organisms**: Dialog (+ DialogHeader, DialogContent, DialogActions, DialogButton, DialogClose, DialogImageHeader, HiddenDialogTitle), OptionCard (+ OptionCardGroup), Table (+ TableHeader, TableBody, TableFooter, TableHead, TableRow, TableCell)

## Icons

Bundled: `i-bolt:check`, `i-bolt:chevron-down`, `i-bolt:chevron-up`, `i-bolt:minus`, `i-svg-spinners:90-ring-with-bg`. Other icons via UnoCSS icon preset (Phosphor: `i-ph:*`).

components--atoms.full:
# Components - Atoms

Atoms are simple, standalone components. Import from `@blitz/design-system`.

## Alert

Feedback banner for inline notifications.

```tsx
import { Alert } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Alert
  type="success"
  title="Operation complete"
  onClose={() => {}}
>
  Your changes have been saved.
</Alert>
```

### AlertProps

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `title` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Alert heading |
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Alert body content |
| `actions` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Action buttons rendered below content |
| `alignActions` | `'start' \| 'center' \| 'end'` | â€” | Alignment of actions |
| `direction` | `'column' \| 'row'` | â€” | Layout direction |
| `type` | `'neutral' \| 'info' \| 'success' \| 'warning' \| 'danger'` | `'neutral'` | Visual style variant |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | Root class override |
| `iconClassName` | `string` | â€” | Icon class override |
| `titleClassName` | `string` | â€” | Title class override |
| `contentClassName` | `string` | â€” | Content class override |
| `onClose` | `() => void` | â€” | Callback when close button clicked |

---

## Avatar

User profile picture with fallback options.

```tsx
import { Avatar } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Avatar
  user={{ name: 'Jane Smith', avatar: '/profile.png' }}
  size="md"
  type="circle"
  fallback="initials"
/>
```

### AvatarProps

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `user` | `{ name?: string; avatar?: string }` | â€” | User data |
| `size` | `'xxs' \| 'xs' \| 'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg' \| 'xl' \| '2xl'` | `'md'` | Avatar dimensions |
| `type` | `'circle' \| 'rounded'` | `'circle'` | Shape |
| `fallback` | `'icon' \| 'initials'` | `'icon'` | What to show when no avatar image |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | Class override |

---

## Badge

Small label pill for status or category.

```tsx
import { Badge } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Badge type="success">Active</Badge>
<Badge type="danger" icon="i-ph:warning" />
```

### BadgeProps

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `type` | `'neutral' \| 'brand' \| 'success' \| 'warning' \| 'danger'` | `'neutral'` | Color variant |
| `icon` | `string` | â€” | UnoCSS icon class. When provided, renders a circular icon badge instead of a text pill |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | Class override |

---

## Button

Primary interactive element. Supports variants, sizes, icons, tooltips, and loading state.

```tsx
import { Button } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Button variant="primary" size="md" onClick={() => {}}>Click Me</Button>
<Button variant="secondary" startIcon="i-ph:plus" loading={isLoading}>Add Item</Button>
<Button variant="ghost" icon="i-ph:trash" tint="danger" />
```

### ButtonProps

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `'primary' \| 'secondary' \| 'tint' \| 'ghost' \| 'high-contrast' \| 'link-gray' \| 'link-color'` | `'primary'` | Visual style |
| `size` | `'lg' \| 'md' \| 'sm' \| 'xs'` | `'md'` | Button dimensions |
| `icon` | `string` | â€” | UnoCSS icon class for icon-only button |
| `startIcon` | `string` | â€” | Icon before label |
| `endIcon` | `string` | â€” | Icon after label |
| `tint` | `'brand' \| 'success' \| 'warning' \| 'danger'` | â€” | Color tint (used with `tint` variant) |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Show loading spinner |
| `loader` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Custom loader element |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disabled state |
| `asChild` | `boolean` | `false` | Render as child element (Radix Slot) |
| `truncate` | `boolean` | `false` | Truncate label text |
| `type` | `'button' \| 'submit' \| 'reset'` | `'button'` | HTML button type |
| `iconClassName` | `string` | â€” | Icon class override |
| `tooltip` | `ButtonTooltip` | â€” | Tooltip configuration |

`ButtonTooltip`: `{ content: ReactNode; side?: Side; align?: Align; sideOffset?: number; maxWidth?: number }`

---

## Checkbox

Boolean toggle built on Radix Checkbox.

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Checkbox
  checked={isChecked}
  onChange={(checked) => setIsChecked(checked === true)}
/>
```

### CheckboxProps

Extends Radix `CheckboxProps` with:

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `onChange` | `(checked: CheckedState) => void` | Change handler. `CheckedState` is `boolean \| 'indeterminate'` |

All other Radix Checkbox props (e.g., `defaultChecked`, `disabled`, `required`, `name`, `value`) are also supported.

---

## Dropdown

Context menu with a customizable trigger and item list.

```tsx
import { Button, Dropdown } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Dropdown trigger={<Button variant="secondary">Options</Button>}>
  <Dropdown.Item onClick={() => console.log('edit')}>Edit</Dropdown.Item>
  <Dropdown.Item onClick={() => console.log('delete')} disabled>Delete</Dropdown.Item>
</Dropdown>
```

### DropdownProps

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `trigger` | `ReactNode \| (({ open }) => ReactNode)` | Trigger element or render function |
| `open` | `boolean` | Controlled open state |
| `onOpenChange` | `(isOpen: boolean) => void` | Open state change handler |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | Disable the trigger |
| `contentProps` | `DropdownMenuContentProps` | Radix content props (alignment, side, etc.) |

### Dropdown.Item Props

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `onClick` | `() => void` | Click handler |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | Disable the item |
| `className` | `string` | Class override |

---

## IconDecoration

Styled container for displaying an icon with background shape and color.

```tsx
import { IconDecoration } from '@blitz/design-system';

<IconDecoration
  icon="i-ph:star"
  variant="brand"
  size="md"
  shape="squircle"
/>
```

### IconDecorationProps

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `icon` | `string` | â€” | **Required.** UnoCSS icon class |
| `variant` | `'default' \| 'brand' \| 'success' \| 'warning' \| 'danger' \| 'neutral'` | `'default'` | Color variant |
| `size` | `'xs' \| 'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg' \| 'xl'` | `'md'` | Container size |
| `shape` | `'rounded' \| 'squircle' \| 'circle'` | `'rounded'` | Container shape |
| `iconClassName` | `string` | â€” | Class override for icon element |

---

## Input

Text input field with label, hint, error, and warning states.

```tsx
import { Input } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Input
  label="Email"
  placeholder="you@example.com"
  hint="We'll never share your email"
  value={email}
  onValueChange={setEmail}
/>

<Input
  label="Username"
  error="Username is taken"
  size="sm"
/>
```

### InputProps

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Label above the input |
| `placeholder` | `string` | â€” | Input placeholder |
| `variant` | `'vertical' \| 'horizontal'` | `'vertical'` | Layout variant |
| `size` | `'md' \| 'sm'` | `'md'` | Input height |
| `error` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Error message (shown below input) |
| `warning` | `boolean` | `false` | Warning styling state |
| `hint` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Helper text below input |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disabled state |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string) => void` | â€” | Value change handler |
| `inputClassName` | `string` | â€” | Class override for inner input |
| `labelTooltip` | `string` | â€” | Tooltip text shown next to label |
| `labelTooltipSurface` | `'1' \| '2' \| '3'` | â€” | Surface level for label tooltip |
| `errorClassName` | `string` | â€” | Class override for error element |

Also supports all standard HTML `<input>` attributes.

---

## Select

Dropdown select with label, placeholder, helper, and error support.

```tsx
import { Select } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Select
  label="Country"
  options={[
    { value: 'us', label: 'United States' },
    { value: 'uk', label: 'United Kingdom' },
  ]}
  placeholder="Select a country"
  onValueChange={(val) => setCountry(val)}
/>
```

### SelectProps

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `options` | `Option[]` | â€” | **Required.** `{ value: string; label: ReactNode; labelDetails?: ReactNode }[]` |
| `value` | `string` | â€” | Controlled value |
| `defaultValue` | `string` | â€” | Initial value |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string) => void` | â€” | Value change handler |
| `label` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Label above the select |
| `labelPosition` | `'top' \| 'side'` | `'top'` | Label placement |
| `placeholder` | `string` | â€” | Placeholder option text |
| `helper` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Helper text below select |
| `error` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Error message |
| `size` | `'xl' \| 'lg' \| 'md'` | `'md'` | Select height |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disabled state |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Loading state |

---

## Switch

Toggle switch built on Radix Switch.

```tsx
import { Switch } from '@blitz/design-system';

<Switch
  checked={isEnabled}
  onCheckedChange={setIsEnabled}
  size="md"
/>
```

### SwitchProps

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `checked` | `boolean` | â€” | Controlled checked state |
| `onCheckedChange` | `(checked: boolean) => void` | â€” | Change handler |
| `size` | `'sm' \| 'md'` | `'md'` | Switch dimensions |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disabled state |
| `id` | `string` | â€” | HTML id (for label association) |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | Root class override |
| `thumbClassName` | `string` | â€” | Thumb class override |
| `style` | `CSSProperties` | â€” | Inline style |

---

## Tooltip

Hover tooltip built on Radix Tooltip. Uses namespace export pattern.

```tsx
import * as Tooltip from '@blitz/design-system';
// Note: Tooltip is exported as a namespace

<Tooltip.Provider>
  <Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Trigger asChild>
      <button>Hover me</button>
    </Tooltip.Trigger>
    <Tooltip.Content side="top" sideOffset={4}>
      Tooltip text
    </Tooltip.Content>
  </Tooltip.Root>
</Tooltip.Provider>
```

### TooltipContent Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | **Required.** Tooltip content |
| `truncate` | `boolean` | `false` | Truncate long content |
| `hideArrow` | `boolean` | `false` | Hide the arrow pointer |
| `maxWidth` | `string \| number` | â€” | Max width of tooltip |
| `surface` | `'1' \| '2' \| '3'` | â€” | Surface level (background depth) |
| `side` | `'top' \| 'right' \| 'bottom' \| 'left'` | `'top'` | Preferred side |
| `align` | `'start' \| 'center' \| 'end'` | `'center'` | Alignment |
| `sideOffset` | `number` | â€” | Offset from trigger |
| `alignOffset` | `number` | â€” | Alignment offset |
| `avoidCollisions` | `boolean` | `true` | Flip when near viewport edge |
| `collisionPadding` | `number` | â€” | Padding from viewport edges |

Re-exports from Radix: `Arrow`, `Portal`, `Provider`, `Root`, `Trigger`.

---

## Skeleton

Placeholder loading component listed in the documentation but not yet exported from the package. This is marked as a Work In Progress in the docs site. Do not use until available in the published package.

# Components - Atoms

All imported from `@blitz/design-system`.

## Alert
`type`: `'neutral'|'info'|'success'|'warning'|'danger'`. Props: `title`, `children`, `actions`, `alignActions`, `direction`, `onClose`, `className`.

## Avatar
`size`: `'xxs'|'xs'|'sm'|'md'|'lg'|'xl'|'2xl'`. `type`: `'circle'|'rounded'`. `fallback`: `'icon'|'initials'`. `user`: `{name?, avatar?}`.

## Badge
`type`: `'neutral'|'brand'|'success'|'warning'|'danger'`. `icon`: UnoCSS icon class (circular icon badge).

## Button
`variant`: `'primary'|'secondary'|'tint'|'ghost'|'high-contrast'|'link-gray'|'link-color'`. `size`: `'lg'|'md'|'sm'|'xs'`. `tint`: `'brand'|'success'|'warning'|'danger'`. `startIcon`, `endIcon`, `icon`: UnoCSS icon classes. `loading`, `disabled`, `asChild`, `tooltip`.

## Checkbox
Extends Radix Checkbox. `onChange: (checked: CheckedState) => void`. `CheckedState = boolean | 'indeterminate'`.

## Dropdown
`trigger`: ReactNode or render function. `Dropdown.Item`: `onClick`, `disabled`, `className`. `open`, `onOpenChange`, `contentProps`.

## IconDecoration
`icon`: required UnoCSS class. `variant`: `'default'|'brand'|'success'|'warning'|'danger'|'neutral'`. `size`: `'xs'|'sm'|'md'|'lg'|'xl'`. `shape`: `'rounded'|'squircle'|'circle'`.

## Input
`label`, `placeholder`, `variant`: `'vertical'|'horizontal'`, `size`: `'md'|'sm'`, `error`, `warning`, `hint`, `onValueChange`, `labelTooltip`, `disabled`.

## Select
`options`: `{value, label, labelDetails?}[]`. `value`, `onValueChange`, `label`, `labelPosition`: `'top'|'side'`, `placeholder`, `helper`, `error`, `size`: `'xl'|'lg'|'md'`, `loading`.

## Switch
Radix Switch. `checked`, `onCheckedChange`, `size`: `'sm'|'md'`, `disabled`.

## Tooltip
Namespace export: `import * as Tooltip from '@blitz/design-system'`. Use `Provider > Root > Trigger + Content`. `Content` props: `side`, `align`, `sideOffset`, `hideArrow`, `maxWidth`, `surface`.

## Skeleton
Work In Progress. Not yet exported from package. Do not use until available.

Falls back to default icon per badge type | | `onBack` | `VoidFunction` | Show back arrow button (for multi-step dialogs) | | `noBorder` | `boolean` | Remove border between header and content | | `unstyled` | `boolean` | Remove all default styles | | `className` | `string` | Class override | | `wrapperClassName` | `string` | Wrapper class override | ### DialogImageHeader Props | Prop | Type | Description | |------|------|-------------| | `src` | `string` | **Required.** Image URL | | `title` | `ReactNode` | Title below image | | `description` | `ReactNode` | Description below title | ### DialogContent Props | Prop | Type | Description | |------|------|-------------| | `children` | `ReactNode` | **Required.** Content | | `className` | `string` | Class override | | `unstyled` | `boolean` | Remove default text color/size styles | | `noPadding` | `boolean` | Remove default `p-6` padding | ### DialogActions Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | |------|------|---------|-------------| | `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | **Required.** Action buttons | | `orientation` | `'horizontal' \| 'vertical'` | `'horizontal'` | Button layout | | `actionSize` | `'compact' \| 'fullwidth'` | `'compact'` | `compact` = hug content; `fullwidth` = equal width buttons | | `className` | `string` | â€” | Class override | ### DialogButton Same props as `Button`. items.name: Button
items.docs: # Button Interactive button component with multiple variants, sizes, icon support, and loading state. Built with Radix UI Slot for polymorphic rendering. ## Import ```tsx import { Button } from '@blitz/design-system'; ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | |------|------|---------|-------------| | `variant` | `'primary' \| 'secondary' \| 'tint' \| 'ghost' \| 'high-contrast' \| 'link-gray' \| 'link-color'` | `'primary'` | Visual style variant | | `size` | `'lg' \| 'md' \| 'sm' \| 'xs'` | `'lg'` | Button size (also accepts `'icon-xs'`, `'icon-sm'`, `'icon-md'`, `'icon-lg'` for icon-only buttons) | | `tint` | `'brand' \| 'success' \| 'warning' \| 'danger'` | `'brand'` | Color tint for `tint`, `primary`, and `link-color` variants | | `icon` | `string` | â€” | Icon-only mode: CSS icon class (e.g. `i-ph:plus`). Converts size to icon size. | | `startIcon` | `string` | â€” | CSS icon class to show at the start of the button | | `endIcon` | `string` | â€” | CSS icon class to show at the end of the button | | `iconClassName` | `string` | â€” | Additional className for icons | | `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables the button | | `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Shows loading spinner, disables interaction | | `loader` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Custom loader element (replaces default spinner) | | `truncate` | `boolean` | `true` | Truncates long text with ellipsis | | `asChild` | `boolean` | â€” | Renders as child element using Radix Slot | | `type` | `'button' \| 'submit' \| 'reset'` | `'button'` | HTML button type | | `tooltip` | `ButtonTooltip` | â€” | Tooltip configuration object | | `className` | `string` | â€” | Additional CSS classes | ### ButtonTooltip ```ts interface ButtonTooltip { content: ReactNode; side?: 'top' | 'right' | 'bottom' | 'left'; align?: 'start' | 'center' | 'end'; sideOffset?: number; maxWidth?: number; } ``` ## Variants - **primary**: Brand-colored filled button. Combine with `tint` for success/warning/danger colors. - **secondary**: Soft inverted-surface background. - **tint**: Semi-transparent tinted background with colored text. Use `tint` prop to pick color. - **ghost**: Transparent background, reveals on hover. - **high-contrast**: Inverse surface for high contrast scenarios. - **link-gray**: Text-only link style with secondary text color. - **link-color**: Text-only link style, colored. Use `tint` prop to pick color. ## Sizes - **xs**: Height 28px, text-xs, padding px-1.5 - **sm**: Height 32px, text-xs, padding px-2 - **md**: Height 36px, text-sm, padding px-3 - **lg** (default): Height 40px, text-sm, padding px-4 Icon-only sizes (square): `icon-xs` 28px, `icon-sm` 32px, `icon-md` 36px, `icon-lg` 40px. ## Usage Examples ```tsx // Basic primary button // Secondary button, medium size // Danger tint // Ghost button with start icon // Icon-only button // With tooltip // Tint variants ``` ## Accessibility - Buttons are focusable by default. - Use `tabIndex={-1}` for buttons outside the tab order. - Disabled buttons are automatically removed from tab order. - Always provide `aria-label` for icon-only buttons.

items.name: Input
items.docs:
# Input

Text input field with optional label, hint, error/warning states, and tooltip support. Supports both vertical and horizontal layouts.

## Import

```tsx
import { Input } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `label` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Label displayed above or beside the input |
| `placeholder` | `string` | â€” | Placeholder text |
| `variant` | `'vertical' \| 'horizontal'` | `'vertical'` | Layout: vertical stacks label above; horizontal places label to the side |
| `size` | `'md' \| 'sm'` | `'md'` | Input size. `md` = height 44px, text-base; `sm` = height 40px, text-sm |
| `error` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Error message. When set, border turns danger-colored. If `warning` is also set, shows warning color instead |
| `warning` | `boolean` | â€” | Modifier for `error` state to show warning color instead of danger |
| `hint` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Helper text shown below input when no error is present |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables the input |
| `inputClassName` | `string` | â€” | Additional className for the inner `<input>` element |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | Additional className for the outer wrapper |
| `errorClassName` | `string` | â€” | Additional className for the error/hint text |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string) => void` | â€” | Fires with the string value on every change (convenience wrapper around `onChange`) |
| `labelTooltip` | `string` | â€” | Tooltip text shown on the label question mark icon |
| `labelTooltipSurface` | `'1' \| '2' \| '3'` | â€” | Tooltip surface variant |
| `id` | `string` | â€” | ID for the input (auto-generated if not provided for label association) |
| All native `<input>` props | â€” | â€” | e.g. `type`, `value`, `onChange`, `readOnly`, `autoFocus`, etc. |

## Usage Examples

```tsx
// Basic input with label
<Input label="Email" placeholder="you@example.com" type="email" />

// With error state
<Input
  label="Password"
  type="password"
  error="Password must be at least 8 characters"
/>

// With warning state
<Input
  label="Username"
  error="This username may already be taken"
  warning
/>

// With hint
<Input
  label="Username"
  hint="Only letters, numbers, and underscores"
  placeholder="johndoe"
/>

// Small size
<Input label="Search" size="sm" placeholder="Search..." />

// Horizontal layout
<Input
  label="Name"
  variant="horizontal"
  placeholder="John Doe"
/>

// Controlled with onValueChange
<Input
  label="Email"
  value={email}
  onValueChange={setEmail}
/>

// With label tooltip
<Input
  label="API Key"
  labelTooltip="Your secret API key. Keep this safe!"
  type="password"
/>
```

## States

- **Default**: `border-bolt-ds-borderOutline`, focus ring `bolt-ds-brandHighlight`
- **Error**: `border-bolt-ds-danger`, focus ring `bolt-ds-dangerHighlight`
- **Warning**: `border-bolt-ds-warning`, focus ring `bolt-ds-warningHighlight`
- **Disabled**: 75% opacity, `cursor-not-allowed`
- **Read-only**: Tertiary text color

items.name: Alert
items.docs:
# Alert

Contextual feedback message component for user actions and important information. Supports five semantic types, optional title, body content, action buttons, and a dismissible close button.

## Import

```tsx
import { Alert } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `type` | `'neutral' \| 'info' \| 'success' \| 'warning' \| 'danger'` | `'neutral'` | Semantic type controlling colors and icon |
| `title` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Optional title text shown prominently |
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Body content / description |
| `actions` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Action buttons or links |
| `alignActions` | `'start' \| 'center' \| 'end'` | `'start'` | Alignment of action buttons |
| `direction` | `'column' \| 'row'` | `'column'` | Layout of content + actions: `column` stacks actions below; `row` places them on the right |
| `onClose` | `() => void` | â€” | If provided, shows an X close button in the top-right corner |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | Additional className for the root element |
| `iconClassName` | `string` | â€” | Override the default icon with a CSS icon class |
| `titleClassName` | `string` | â€” | Additional className for the title |
| `contentClassName` | `string` | â€” | Additional className for the content area |

## Types and Icons

| Type | Icon | Colors |
|------|------|--------|
| `neutral` | `i-ph:star` | `bg-bolt-ds-neutralContainer`, border `bolt-ds-neutralOutline` |
| `info` | `i-ph:info` | `bg-bolt-ds-brandContainer`, border `bolt-ds-brandOutline` |
| `success` | `i-ph:check-circle` | `bg-bolt-ds-successContainer`, border `bolt-ds-successOutline` |
| `warning` | `i-ph:warning` | `bg-bolt-ds-warningContainer`, border `bolt-ds-warningOutline` |
| `danger` | `i-ph:warning-circle` | `bg-bolt-ds-dangerContainer`, border `bolt-ds-dangerOutline` |

## Usage Examples

```tsx
// Basic alert
<Alert title="Success" type="success">
  Your changes have been saved.
</Alert>

// Without title (description only)
<Alert type="info">
  This section is under construction.
</Alert>

// With action buttons (column layout)
<Alert
  title="Session Expired"
  type="danger"
  actions={
    <>
      <Button size="sm" variant="primary" tint="danger">Log in again</Button>
      <Button size="sm" variant="ghost">Get help</Button>
    </>
  }
>
  Your session has expired. Please log in again.
</Alert>

// Row layout - actions on the right
<Alert
  type="warning"
  title="Update available"
  direction="row"
  actions={<Button size="sm">Update now</Button>}
>
  A new version is ready to install.
</Alert>

// Dismissible alert
<Alert type="neutral" title="Notice" onClose={() => setVisible(false)}>
  Click the X to dismiss this alert.
</Alert>

// Custom icon
<Alert
  type="info"
  iconClassName="i-ph:rocket"
  title="New Feature"
>
  We've just launched a new feature!
</Alert>
```

## When to Use

- Form validation feedback
- Page-level status messages
- Contextual warnings within content
- Feature announcements in specific sections

Use toast notifications for system-wide or ephemeral messages instead.

# Avatar User avatar component that displays a profile image, initials fallback, or a default user icon. Automatically falls back when an image fails to load. ## Import ```tsx import { Avatar } from '@blitz/design-system'; ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | |------|------|---------|-------------| | `user` | `{ name?: string; avatar?: string }` | â€” | User data. `avatar` is the image URL; `name` is used to derive initials | | `size` | `'xxs' \| 'xs' \| 'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg' \| 'xl' \| '2xl'` | `'sm'` | Avatar size | | `type` | `'circle' \| 'rounded'` | `'circle'` | Shape: circle or rounded rectangle (radius scales with size) | | `fallback` | `'icon' \| 'initials'` | `'icon'` | What to show when no image is available | | `className` | `string` | â€” | Additional CSS classes | ## Sizes | Size | Dimensions | |------|------------| | `xxs` | 16x16px | | `xs` | 24x24px | | `sm` | 32x32px | | `md` | 36x36px | | `lg` | 48x48px | | `xl` | 56x56px | | `2xl` | 64x64px | ## Fallback Logic 1. If `user.avatar` URL is provided and loads successfully, shows the image. 2. On image error, falls back according to `fallback` prop: - `'initials'`: Extracts initials from `user.name` (up to 2 characters, first letter of first and last word). - `'icon'`: Shows a generic user icon (`i-ph:user`). 3. If no `user` is provided, always shows the fallback. ## Usage Examples ```tsx // With image // Initials fallback // Renders 'JD' // Icon fallback (default) // No user data // Rounded shape // Extra small // In a user profile header

Jane Doe

jane@example.com

``` ## Notes - Initials are automatically derived: "Jane Doe" â†’ "JD", "Alice" â†’ "A". - The component renders as a `
` containing either ``, `` (initials), or icon ``. - Background is `bg-bolt-ds-surfaceTwo` and border is `border-bolt-ds-borderOutline` when showing an image.

items.docs:
# Badge

A small label component for displaying status, categories, or counts. Renders as either a text pill or a circular icon badge.

## Import

```tsx
import { Badge } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|------|------|---------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | `'neutral' \| 'brand' \| 'success' \| 'warning' \| 'danger'` | `'neutral'` | No | Semantic color variant |
| `icon` | `string` | â€” | No | UnoCSS icon class. When provided, renders a circular icon badge instead of a text pill |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Additional CSS classes |
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | No | Badge text content (used when no icon is provided) |

## Variants

Five semantic types for status and categorization:
- `neutral` â€” `bg-bolt-ds-surfaceTwo text-bolt-ds-textPrimary`
- `brand` â€” `bg-bolt-ds-brandContainer text-bolt-ds-brand`
- `success` â€” `bg-bolt-ds-successContainer text-bolt-ds-success`
- `warning` â€” `bg-bolt-ds-warningContainer text-bolt-ds-warning`
- `danger` â€” `bg-bolt-ds-dangerContainer text-bolt-ds-danger`

## Usage

### Text pill badge
```tsx
<Badge type="success">Active</Badge>
<Badge type="danger">Error</Badge>
<Badge type="warning">Pending</Badge>
<Badge type="brand">New</Badge>
<Badge>Neutral</Badge>
```

### Icon badge (circular)
```tsx
// When `icon` is provided, renders as a 48x48 circular badge
<Badge type="success" icon="i-heroicons:check-circle" />
<Badge type="danger" icon="i-heroicons:exclamation-circle" />
<Badge type="brand" icon="i-heroicons:sparkles" />
```

## Notes
- Text pill: `inline-flex`, rounded-full, `px-2.5 py-0.5`, min-width 56px, text-xs font-medium
- Icon badge: `size-12` (48x48), rounded-full, text-xl
- Icon classes use the UnoCSS icon system (e.g., `i-heroicons:*`, `i-ph:*`)

# Dropdown

A composable dropdown menu built on Radix UI's DropdownMenu primitive. Supports controlled and uncontrolled open state, a render-prop trigger, and disabled items.

## Import

```tsx
import { Dropdown } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

## Props

### Dropdown

| Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|------|------|---------|----------|-------------|
| `trigger` | `ReactNode \| (({ open }: { open: boolean }) => ReactNode)` | â€” | Yes | Trigger element or render function receiving open state |
| `contentProps` | `DropdownMenuContentProps` | â€” | No | Props passed through to the Radix Content element |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | â€” | No | Disables the trigger |
| `open` | `boolean` | â€” | No | Controlled open state |
| `onOpenChange` | `(isOpen: boolean) => void` | â€” | No | Callback when open state changes |
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | No | Dropdown.Item elements |

### Dropdown.Item

| Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|------|------|---------|----------|-------------|
| `onClick` | `() => void` | â€” | No | Click handler |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | â€” | No | Disables the item |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Additional CSS classes |
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | No | Item content |

## Usage

### Basic
```tsx
<Dropdown trigger={<Button>Open Menu</Button>}>
  <Dropdown.Item onClick={() => console.log('Edit')}>Edit</Dropdown.Item>
  <Dropdown.Item onClick={() => console.log('Duplicate')}>Duplicate</Dropdown.Item>
  <Dropdown.Item onClick={() => console.log('Delete')}>Delete</Dropdown.Item>
</Dropdown>
```

### Trigger as function (receives open state)
```tsx
<Dropdown
  trigger={({ open }) => (
    <Button endIcon={open ? 'i-bolt:chevron-up' : 'i-bolt:chevron-down'}>
      Actions
    </Button>
  )}
>
  <Dropdown.Item>View</Dropdown.Item>
  <Dropdown.Item>Edit</Dropdown.Item>
</Dropdown>
```

### With disabled items
```tsx
<Dropdown trigger={<Button>Actions</Button>}>
  <Dropdown.Item onClick={handleEdit}>Edit</Dropdown.Item>
  <Dropdown.Item disabled>Export (unavailable)</Dropdown.Item>
  <Dropdown.Item onClick={handleDelete}>Delete</Dropdown.Item>
</Dropdown>
```

### Controlled open state
```tsx
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

<Dropdown
  open={open}
  onOpenChange={setOpen}
  trigger={<Button>Controlled</Button>}
>
  <Dropdown.Item onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>Close me</Dropdown.Item>
</Dropdown>
```

## Notes
- Built on `@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu`
- Content uses `sideOffset={4}`, animated open/close transitions
- Content width is at minimum the trigger's width (`min-w-[--radix-dropdown-menu-trigger-width]`)
- Content max height respects available space (`max-h-[--radix-dropdown-menu-content-available-height]`)
- Items styled with `hover:bg-bolt-ds-surfaceHighlight`, disabled items shown at 50% opacity

items.docs:
# Tooltip

A composable tooltip built on Radix UI's Tooltip primitive. Re-exports `Arrow`, `Portal`, `Provider`, `Root`, and `Trigger` from `@radix-ui/react-tooltip` with a custom styled `Content` component.

## Import

```tsx
import { Provider, Root, Trigger, Portal, Arrow, Content } from '@blitz/design-system/Tooltip';
// or via named re-export from Tooltip module:
import * as Tooltip from '@blitz/design-system';
// Tooltip.Provider, Tooltip.Root, Tooltip.Trigger, Tooltip.Content, etc.
```

## Components

### Content (custom)

The only custom component â€” provides Bolt DS styling.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Tooltip content |
| `truncate` | `boolean` | â€” | Truncate long content |
| `hideArrow` | `boolean` | â€” | Hide the arrow pointer |
| `maxWidth` | `string \| number` | â€” | Constrain content width |
| `surface` | `'1' \| '2' \| '3'` | â€” | Surface level matching the trigger container elevation |
| `side` | `'top' \| 'right' \| 'bottom' \| 'left'` | `'top'` | Placement side |
| `align` | `'start' \| 'center' \| 'end'` | `'center'` | Alignment relative to trigger |
| `sideOffset` | `number` | â€” | Distance from trigger |
| `alignOffset` | `number` | â€” | Alignment offset |
| `avoidCollisions` | `boolean` | â€” | Auto-flip when near viewport edge |
| `collisionPadding` | `number \| object` | â€” | Padding around viewport for collision detection |

### Provider (from Radix)

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `delayDuration` | `number` | `700` | Delay in ms before tooltip opens |
| `skipDelayDuration` | `number` | `300` | Time to skip delay when quickly moving between triggers |
| `disableHoverableContent` | `boolean` | `false` | Prevent hovering into tooltip content from keeping it open |

### Root (from Radix)

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `open` | `boolean` | â€” | Controlled open state |
| `defaultOpen` | `boolean` | â€” | Uncontrolled initial open state |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` | â€” | Open state callback |
| `delayDuration` | `number` | `700` | Overrides Provider delay |
| `disableHoverableContent` | `boolean` | `false` | Overrides Provider setting |

### Trigger (from Radix)

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `asChild` | `boolean` | Merge props onto child element |

## Usage

### Basic tooltip
```tsx
import { Provider, Root, Trigger, Portal, Content } from '@blitz/design-system/Tooltip';

<Provider>
  <Root>
    <Trigger asChild>
      <Button>Hover me</Button>
    </Trigger>
    <Portal>
      <Content side="top">
        Helpful tooltip text
      </Content>
    </Portal>
  </Root>
</Provider>
```

### Position variants
```tsx
// side can be: top, right, bottom, left
<Content side="bottom" sideOffset={6}>Bottom tooltip</Content>
<Content side="right" align="start">Right-aligned</Content>
```

### Surface levels
```tsx
// Match surface to the container's elevation
<Content surface="1">Surface 1 tooltip</Content>
<Content surface="2">Surface 2 tooltip</Content>
<Content surface="3">Surface 3 tooltip</Content>
```

### Without arrow and with max width
```tsx
<Content hideArrow maxWidth={200}>
  This tooltip has no arrow and is constrained to 200px width.
</Content>
```

### Wrap Provider at app or section level
```tsx
// Place Provider high in the tree to share tooltip behavior
<Provider delayDuration={300}>
  <App />
</Provider>
```

## Notes
- `Provider` should wrap usage context (can be app root or section)
- `Portal` renders the tooltip outside the DOM hierarchy to avoid overflow clipping
- `Trigger asChild` merges tooltip behavior onto the child element without adding a wrapper DOM node
- The `Button` component has a built-in `tooltip` prop for convenience (see Button docs)

Includes sub-components for composable layout: header (text or image), content, and actions. ## Import ```tsx import { Dialog, DialogHeader, DialogImageHeader, DialogContent, DialogActions, DialogButton, DialogClose, HiddenDialogTitle, } from '@blitz/design-system'; ``` ## Sub-components ### Dialog The root container. | â€” | No | When true, disables outside interaction | | `unstyled` | `boolean` | `false` | No | Remove default border/background/shadow | | `portalContainer` | `Element` | â€” | No | Custom portal container | | `contentClassName` | `string` | â€” | No | Class for inner content wrapper | | `backdropClassName` | `string` | â€” | No | Class for the backdrop overlay | | `closeButtonClassName` | `string` | `''` | No | Class for the close button | | `closeButtonProps` | `ButtonProps` | â€” | No | Props for the close button | | `data-testid` | `string` | â€” | No | Test ID | ### DialogHeader Header section with optional badge, title, description, and back button. | Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description | |------|------|---------|----------|-------------| | `title` | `ReactNode` | â€” | No | Dialog title (rendered as accessible heading) | | `description` | `ReactNode` | â€” | No | Description rendered below title | | `badge` | `BadgeType` | â€” | No | Color variant for the icon badge above title | | `badgeIcon` | `string` | â€” | No | UnoCSS icon class for the badge. Falls back to default icon per badge type | | `onBack` | `VoidFunction` | â€” | No | Shows a back arrow button. Useful for multi-step dialogs | | `noBorder` | `boolean` | `false` | No | Remove bottom border between header and content | | `unstyled` | `boolean` | `false` | No | Remove all default header styles | | `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Class for inner header div | | `wrapperClassName` | `string` | â€” | No | Class for the title/description wrapper | ### DialogImageHeader Header with a full-width background image above the title and description. | Prop | Type | Required | Description | |------|------|----------|-------------| | `src` | `string` | Yes | Image URL | | `title` | `ReactNode` | No | Dialog title | | `description` | `ReactNode` | No | Description | ### DialogContent Scrollable content area. Maps to Radix `Dialog.Description`. | Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description | |------|------|---------|----------|-------------| | `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Yes | Content | | `unstyled` | `boolean` | `false` | No | Remove default text styles | | `noPadding` | `boolean` | `false` | No | Remove default `p-6` padding | | `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Additional CSS classes | ### DialogActions Action button container (usually at the bottom). | Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description | |------|------|---------|----------|-------------| | `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Yes | Button elements | | `orientation` | `'horizontal' \| 'vertical'` | `'horizontal'` | No | Button layout direction | | `actionSize` | `'compact' \| 'fullwidth'` | `'compact'` | No | `compact`: buttons hug content; `fullwidth`: buttons share width equally | | `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Additional CSS classes | ### DialogButton A `Button` pre-configured for dialog actions. Accepts all `ButtonProps` â€” see Button documentation. ### DialogClose Re-export of `@radix-ui/react-dialog` `Close`. Wrap around any element to make it close the dialog. ### HiddenDialogTitle Provides an accessible title for screen readers without visually rendering it. | Prop | Type | Description | |------|------|-------------| | `children` | `ReactNode` | Title text (visually hidden) | ## Usage ### Basic dialog ```tsx const [open, setOpen] = useState(false); setOpen(false)} maxWidth={480}>

This action cannot be undone.

 setOpen(false)}>Cancel Delete ``` ### With badge (status dialog) ```tsx setOpen(false)} maxWidth={440}> setOpen(false)}>Cancel Delete ``` ### Multi-step dialog with back button ```tsx setOpen(false)} maxWidth={560}> setStep(1)} /> {/* step content */} setOpen(false)}>Cancel Next ``` ### Image header dialog ```tsx setOpen(false)} maxWidth={520}> Learn more ``` ### Hidden title (accessibility) ```tsx setOpen(false)}> Image preview ``` ## Accessibility | Feature | Behavior | |---------|----------| | Focus trap | Focus is trapped inside the dialog while open. Tab and Shift+Tab cycle through focusable elements. | | Escape to close | Pressing Escape calls `onClose`. Disable with `closeOnEscape={false}`. | | ARIA attributes | `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, and `aria-labelledby` are set automatically via Radix. | | Backdrop click | Clicking the backdrop calls `onClose`. Disable with `closeOnBackdropClick={false}`. | | Scroll lock | Body scroll is locked while the dialog is open. | | Hidden title | Use `HiddenDialogTitle` when you need a title for screen readers but don't want `DialogHeader`. | ## Guidelines - Use dialogs sparingly â€” they interrupt flow. Only use for focused attention or confirmation. - Always provide a clear way to close (button, Escape, backdrop). - Keep content concise. If it's very long, consider a dedicated page. - Use `maxWidth` in pixels: 400â€“500 for confirmations, 560â€“640 for forms. - Primary action goes on the right in horizontal layouts. - Use `badge` + `badgeIcon` on `DialogHeader` for status-type dialogs (warning, danger, success). - Pass `onBack` to `DialogHeader` for wizard-style multi-step dialogs. ## Default icon per badge type | Badge type | Default icon | |------------|-------------| | `neutral` | `i-heroicons:information-circle` | | `brand` | `i-heroicons:sparkles` | | `success` | `i-heroicons:check-circle` | | `warning` | `i-heroicons:exclamation-triangle` | | `danger` | `i-heroicons:exclamation-circle` |

# Table A styled HTML table for displaying tabular data. Wraps content in a scrollable container with design-token borders and backgrounds. Composed from semantic sub-components. ## Import ```tsx import { Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableFooter, TableHead, TableRow, TableCell, } from '@blitz/design-system'; ``` ## Sub-components | Component | HTML Element | Description | |-----------|-------------|-------------| | `Table` | `` | Root wrapper. Includes overflow-auto container. | | `TableHeader` | `` | Table header section. Adds bottom border to header rows. | | `TableBody` | `` | Table body section. Removes border from last row. | | `TableFooter` | `` | Table footer with `bg-bolt-ds-surfaceThree/50` background. | | `TableRow` | `` | Row with bottom border and hover transition. | | `TableHead` | `
` | Header cell â€” muted tertiary text, fixed h-12 height, left-aligned. | | `TableCell` | `` | Data cell with `p-4` padding, middle-aligned. | All sub-components forward refs and accept the standard HTML attributes of their underlying element. ## Props ### Table | Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description | |------|------|---------|----------|-------------| | `containerClassName` | `string` | â€” | No | Class for the outer overflow-auto container | | `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Class for the `` element | | ...HTMLTableElement attrs | | | | All standard HTML table attributes | All other sub-components accept the standard HTML attributes for their respective element type plus `className`. ## Styling Details - **Container**: `border border-bolt-ds-borderDivider rounded-md overflow-auto w-full` - **Table**: `w-full caption-bottom text-sm` - **TableHeader**: Applies `border-b border-bolt-ds-borderDivider` to child rows - **TableBody**: Removes border from last child row - **TableFooter**: `border-t border-bolt-ds-borderDivider bg-bolt-ds-surfaceThree/50 font-medium` - **TableRow**: `border-b last:border-b-0 border-bolt-ds-borderDivider transition-colors` - **TableHead**: `bg-bolt-ds-surfaceOne text-xs text-bolt-ds-textTertiary px-4 py-3 border-b border-bolt-ds-borderDivider h-12 text-left` - **TableCell**: `p-4 align-middle` ## Usage ### Basic table ```tsx
Invoice Status Method Amount INV001 Paid Credit Card $250.00 INV002 Pending PayPal $150.00 Total $400.00
``` ### Striped rows ```tsx Name Value {items.map((item, i) => ( {item.name} {item.value} ))}
``` ### Dense variant (compact padding) ```tsx Name Status {rows.map(row => ( {row.name} {row.status} ))}
``` ### Fixed column widths ```tsx ID Name Description {/* ... */}
``` ### With Badge in cells ```tsx Invoice Status INV001 Paid
``` ## Column Sizing Notes - The `Table` component wraps content in `overflow-auto` â€” it scrolls horizontally when columns exceed the viewport. - Never constrain the table to fit narrow containers; let it scroll. - Use explicit `w-[...]` classes on `TableHead` cells to set minimum column widths when needed.

# DefaultLoader

A simple animated spinner component. Used as the default loading indicator inside `Button` when `loading={true}`, but can also be used standalone.

## Import

```tsx
import { DefaultLoader } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|------|------|---------|----------|-------------|
| `size` | `'lg' \| 'md' \| 'sm' \| 'xs'` | â€” | No | Size variant â€” affects scale of the spinner |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Additional CSS classes |

## Size Scale

| Size | Scale Transform |
|------|-----------------|
| `lg` | `scale-150` |
| `md` | `scale-125` |
| `sm` | `scale-115` |
| `xs` | none |

## Usage

### Standalone spinner
```tsx
<DefaultLoader />
```

### With size
```tsx
<DefaultLoader size="lg" />
<DefaultLoader size="md" />
```

### With custom className
```tsx
<DefaultLoader className="text-bolt-ds-brand" />
```

### As Button loader (automatic)
```tsx
// DefaultLoader is used automatically when loading={true} on Button
<Button loading>Saving...</Button>

// Or pass a custom loader to Button:
<Button loading loader={<DefaultLoader size="sm" className="text-white" />}>
  Saving...
</Button>
```

## Notes
- Renders as a `<span>` with class `i-svg-spinners:90-ring-with-bg` (requires UnoCSS icon system)
- The `i-svg-spinners:90-ring-with-bg` icon is an animated SVG spinner
- When used inside `Button` with `loading={true}`, it's positioned absolutely centered and the button label is made invisible (`op-0`), maintaining button width

items.docs:
# OptionCard

A card component for displaying an option with a title, optional description, and an action element. Commonly used inside an `OptionCardGroup` to present a list of settings or choices.

## Import

```tsx
import { OptionCard, OptionCardGroup } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|------|------|---------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | `string` | â€” | Yes | Main label for the option |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | â€” | No | Secondary text or node below the title |
| `action` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Yes | Action element displayed on the right (e.g. Switch, Button, Checkbox) |
| `id` | `string` | â€” | No | HTML id, useful for associating the label with the action control |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Additional CSS classes |

## Usage

```tsx
import { OptionCard, OptionCardGroup, Switch } from '@blitz/design-system';

function Settings() {
  return (
    <OptionCardGroup>
      <OptionCard
        id="notifications"
        title="Notifications"
        description="Receive email notifications"
        action={<Switch id="notifications" />}
      />
      <OptionCard
        id="dark-mode"
        title="Dark Mode"
        description="Use dark color theme"
        action={<Switch id="dark-mode" />}
      />
    </OptionCardGroup>
  );
}
```

## Layout

OptionCard uses a flex row layout with:
- Title and description on the left (min-w-0 for text truncation)
- Action element on the right (shrink-0 to prevent squishing)
- `gap-8` between left and right sections
- `py-4` vertical padding

## Styling

- Title: `text-sm font-medium text-bolt-ds-textPrimary`
- Description: `text-sm text-bolt-ds-textSecondary mt-0.5`

## Related

- Use inside `OptionCardGroup` for divider-separated lists
- Pair with `Switch`, `Checkbox`, or `Button` as the action element

# OptionCardGroup

A container component that wraps multiple `OptionCard` components with divider lines between them.

## Import

```tsx
import { OptionCardGroup } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|------|------|---------|----------|-------------|
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Yes | OptionCard elements to render |
| `className` | `string` | â€” | No | Additional CSS classes |

## Usage

```tsx
import { OptionCard, OptionCardGroup, Switch, Checkbox } from '@blitz/design-system';

function SettingsPanel() {
  return (
    <OptionCardGroup>
      <OptionCard
        title="Auto-save"
        description="Automatically save changes"
        action={<Switch />}
      />
      <OptionCard
        title="Show line numbers"
        action={<Checkbox />}
      />
      <OptionCard
        title="Enable spell check"
        description="Check spelling as you type"
        action={<Switch />}
      />
    </OptionCardGroup>
  );
}
```

## Styling

Applies `divide-y divide-bolt-ds-borderDivider` to create horizontal dividers between child OptionCards.

items.docs:
# BoltDesignSystemProvider

A provider component that wraps the application to supply required context for the Bolt Design System. Currently provides the Radix UI Tooltip provider.

## Import

```tsx
import { BoltDesignSystemProvider } from '@blitz/design-system';
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `children` | `ReactNode` | â€” | Application content |
| `tooltip` | `Omit<TooltipProviderProps, 'children'>` | â€” | Optional tooltip provider configuration (delayDuration, skipDelayDuration, etc.) |

## Usage

Wrap your application root with `BoltDesignSystemProvider` to enable tooltip functionality and any future design system context:

```tsx
import { BoltDesignSystemProvider } from '@blitz/design-system';

function App() {
  return (
    <BoltDesignSystemProvider tooltip={{ delayDuration: 200 }}>
      {/* Your application */}
    </BoltDesignSystemProvider>
  );
}
```

## Notes

- Required when using `Tooltip` or `Button` with `tooltip` prop
- Should be placed near the root of your component tree
- Passes through Radix UI Tooltip.Provider props via the `tooltip` prop
